claim: Raw data - each object is one belief (id, kind, claim, deps). The whole graph is this one file.

evidence.detail: Observed in beliefs/beliefs.json: the graph serializes as a flat JSON array of belief objects, each opening with its id field; there is no other storage. The anchor pins the first id line as the entry into the raw data (explicit @1 - the id key recurs once per belief by design).

claim: The boundary: a JSON map with string keys becomes a typed %Belief{}. Everything downstream works on the struct, not the map.

evidence.detail: Belief.from_map/1 maps every string key of the decoded JSON object onto the %Belief{} struct fields and records the original key set in _keys for byte-stable round-trips. No other entry point converts raw maps to structs.

claim: Loads the whole graph off disk and hands back %Belief{} structs. The single read path the CLI and dashboard share.

evidence.detail: Store.read/0 resolves the graph path via CB.Config, decodes the JSON array, and maps each object through Belief.from_map/1. Every consumer (bs shell, verify tasks, dashboard) reads through this function.

claim: Renders beliefs back out to the terminal (ANSI). The other end of the pipeline from the raw JSON you started at.

evidence.detail: Formatter.table/2 takes %Belief{} structs and emits the ANSI table the bs shell prints - the render end of the data -> struct -> render pipeline.

name: belief-pipeline
claim: The belief-pipeline codepath: a narrated, branching tour of the pipeline from raw data to render (beliefs.json -> Belief.from_map/1 -> Store.read/0 -> Formatter.table/2). Entry and step navigation live in this render-spec per cb:c044; presented interactively by the present-codepath skill and linearly by mix cb.render.codepath.

choices.label: How does raw JSON become a struct?
How is it rendered back out?

invariants: Navigation here is render metadata; reordering this spec supersedes this belief, never the claim beliefs it references.

evidence.detail: Reauthored from the retired standalone instance (codepath/codepaths.json, formerly walkthroughs.json): same four stops and the same entry branch (data forks to from-map or formatter; from-map continues to store). Drafted outside the graph and imported once settled, per the plan-2 authoring loop.
